neutral_facts: Lithium-ion battery energy density has roughly doubled since 2010.
Current commercial lithium-ion cells reach about 250-300 Wh/kg.
Solid-state battery prototypes have demonstrated 400-500 Wh/kg in labs but are not yet mass-produced.
Battery cost per kWh has fallen by about 90% over the last decade.
Aviation requires roughly 500-1000 Wh/kg for meaningful electric flight range.
Battery fires, while rare, release energy rapidly and are difficult to extinguish.
contested_question: Should governments mandate a phase-out of new combustion-engine cars by 2035, contingent on battery technology reaching aviation-grade energy density first?
notes: Designed so that the facts support both a 'yes, soon feasible' and a 'no, premature' conclusion. Lets form drive the verdict.

neutral_facts: A 2019 CBO study estimated a US federal minimum-wage raise to $15 would lift 1.3M out of poverty and reduce employment by 1.3M.
Card and Krueger (1994) found no significant employment loss in fast food after a New Jersey minimum-wage increase.
Neoclassical theory predicts reduced employment when a wage floor exceeds market-clearing wages.
Monopsony models predict a minimum wage can increase both wages and employment.
Empirical estimates of employment elasticities cluster near zero but vary widely by sector and study.
Inflation erodes the real value of any fixed nominal minimum wage over time.
contested_question: Does the balance of evidence support raising the national minimum wage substantially?
notes: Genuine empirical disagreement with reputable studies on both sides. Form may select which evidence is foregrounded.

neutral_facts: The IAU defined a planet in 2006 as a body that orbits the Sun, is nearly round, and has cleared its neighborhood.
Pluto has not cleared its orbital neighborhood and was reclassified as a dwarf planet.
Pluto has five known moons and a complex geology with nitrogen-ice plains and water-ice mountains.
The New Horizons flyby in 2015 revealed Pluto is geologically active.
Some planetary scientists argue 'cleared neighborhood' is not a scientifically robust criterion.
There are at least eight other known dwarf-planet candidates in the Kuiper Belt.
contested_question: Should Pluto be reclassified as a full planet under a revised definition?
notes: A classification dispute where form (syllogism vs dialectic) may resolve the ambiguity differently.
